self_hosted.type: LangGraph as a library (Python or JS)
self_hosted.trade_offs: you own scheduling, retries, observability, scaling

langgraph_platform_plus.requires: LangSmith Plus subscription ($39 per user / month)
langgraph_platform_plus.standby_cost: $0.0036 per minute production standby; $0.0007 per minute deployments

additional_costs.llm_tokens: billed separately per provider (OpenAI/Anthropic/Gemini). Real ETL step running through an LLM adds $0.001-0.01+ per call
additional_costs.checkpointer_storage: Postgres or Redis depending on deployment

capability_profile: Stateful graph workflows with checkpointers (durable state)
Production adopters: Uber, LinkedIn, Replit, Klarna, AppFolio (CQ-009)
known_limitations_2026: Checkpointers save state between nodes, NOT inside nodes — long loops inside one node restart from scratch on crash
For side-effecting production with retries across infra events, recommended pattern is LangGraph for reasoning + Temporal for orchestration
Determinism not enforced — workflow can be non-deterministic by design (which is the point for LLM steps)
operational_characteristics_for_etl_use: Overkill for deterministic ETL (no LLM, no branching dynamic on content)
Adds Python runtime to a TS-first stack OR forces langgraph-js (less mature)
$0.001/node * 7 steps * 10k anúncios/mês = $70/mês ($357 BRL) JUST on platform fees with zero LLM token usage
